signal.headline: Hungary 6 days from pivotal vote as Tisza leads by 19–23 points; Benin election same day; US tariff defiance tests SCOTUS authority
signal.body: Hungary's April 12 parliamentary election is the most consequential single electoral event in the WDM's coverage geography this cycle. Independent polls give Tisza a 19–23 point lead among decided voters; leaked internal Fidesz data also projects Tisza victory. However, electoral redistricting requires a 3–6 point national advantage for a parliamentary majority, and Fidesz's 8 consecutive by-election wins signal rural constituency resilience not captured by national polling. On the same day, Benin's presidential election will confirm whether it crosses the Rapid Decay threshold — only ruling-bloc candidates are approved. In the United States, Trump's Section 122 global surcharge (10–15%) following the February 20 SCOTUS tariff ruling tests whether constitutional constraints on executive power hold under sustained pressure.

key_judgments.judgment: Hungary's April 12 election will be determinative: a Tisza parliamentary majority would represent the most significant democratic reversal of illiberal consolidation in EU history since 2010; a Fidesz retention despite record polling deficits would confirm that constituency gerrymandering and media capture can permanently insulate a governing party from popular will within EU institutional constraints.
key_judgments.source_basis: Medián (58/35%), 21 Kutatóközpont (56/37%), PolitPro aggregate (50.8/47.8%), CSIS scenario analysis, EUobserver institutional analysis, Polymarket (69% Tisza)

key_judgments.judgment: The US pattern of executive-SCOTUS confrontation has entered a new phase: after SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6–3 (Feb 20), the administration immediately deployed Section 122 authority and continues to frame judicial review as illegitimate. This is not episodic — it is a sustained campaign to delegitimise the institutional constraint on executive power that the founding document places on tariff authority.
key_judgments.source_basis: SCOTUS ruling Learning Resources v. Trump (Feb 20), Section 122 proclamation, Reuters judicial interference analysis (Mar 7), Trump v. Barbara oral arguments (Apr 1)

key_judgments.judgment: Benin's April 12 presidential election will, with high probability, produce a ruling-bloc winner — confirming the threshold crossing from Watch List to Rapid Decay. The January 2026 parliamentary elections (109/109 seats to Talon-aligned parties) and the exclusion of credible opposition candidates from the presidential race are structural indicators, not episodic events.
key_judgments.trajectory: Declining — Rapid Decay threshold crossing expected April 12
key_judgments.source_basis: CIVICUS Watchlist March 2026, Wikipedia election documentation, WADR CENA timetable, Atlantic Council analysis

key_judgments.judgment: Georgia's institutional collapse is accelerating beyond the March 2026 law package: the three simultaneous laws (foreign grants criminalised, extremism law, 8-year civil society ban) represent the most concentrated legislative capture event in any EU candidate country since accession processes were formalised. There is no current pathway to reversal under the Kobakhidze government.
key_judgments.source_basis: FIDH March 2026, CIVICUS Watchlist, OSCE Moscow Mechanism (Mar 12), FCW intelligence-digest CMF-001/002

key_judgments.judgment: The authoritarian diffusion pattern documented by Freedom House (54 countries deteriorating vs 35 improving in 2025) and V-Dem (74% of world population in autocracies) is now structurally stable at its worst level since Cold War measurements began. The WDM's 15-country Rapid Decay roster and Watch List expansion to 13 countries reflects this global structural environment, not a measurement artefact.
key_judgments.source_basis: Freedom House Freedom in the World 2026, V-Dem Democracy Report 2026, V-Dem Dataset v16

global_health_snapshot.lead_concern: Global democracy stabilizes after eight years of decline, but US deterioration accelerates; Sub-Saharan Africa remains fragile with military takeovers; institutional capture patterns emerging across multiple regions

resilience_indicators: Judiciary remains functional despite politicization pressure
Media landscape fragmented but not fully captured

electoral_calendar_watch.fimi_risk: CRITICAL — FCW: three simultaneous confirmed operations (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) — highest documented pre-election FIMI density for a single EU election
electoral_calendar_watch.wdm_significance: Defining event — a Tisza win would be the largest reversal of illiberal consolidation within the EU since 2010. A Fidesz retention despite polling deficits would confirm electoral system capture is terminal.
Watch List threshold event — two ruling-bloc candidates only. If Wadagni wins, Benin crosses to Rapid Decay. Post-election crackdown on any protests would be the confirming event.

electoral_calendar_watch.fimi_risk: HIGH — AGM: AI deepfakes confirmed active in US midterm campaigns (Reuters, Mar 28). Stanford Internet Observatory dismantled — primary independent monitoring infrastructure gone.
electoral_calendar_watch.wdm_significance: V-Dem ERT: active autocratisation episode. SAVE America Act (House-passed) would implement DHS mandatory voter roll access. DNI omitted foreign election interference from Worldwide Threats for first time in decade.

flags.headline: Hungary election FIMI saturation — three simultaneous confirmed operations
flags.linkage: FCW intelligence-digest CMF-001 confirms three simultaneous confirmed/assessed Russian operations targeting Hungary April 12 election (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) — highest documented pre-election FIMI density for a single EU election. WDM scores Hungary as Rapid Decay (5.0); the FIMI layer is the constitutive mechanism enabling Fidesz's information environment capture to persist even with record opposition poll leads. ESA dimension: if Orbán retains power with confirmed Russian FIMI support, this consolidates a Russian-aligned veto actor inside the EU for the next parliamentary cycle.

flags.headline: AI deepfakes active in US 2026 midterm campaigns — monitoring infrastructure vacuum
flags.linkage: AGM cross-monitor flag cmf-002 confirmed: AI deepfakes now active in US midterm campaigns (Reuters, Mar 28). Simultaneously, Stanford Internet Observatory — the primary independent monitoring infrastructure for US election disinformation — was dismantled under political pressure. WDM tracks the electoral environment; AGM tracks the capability and governance vacuum; FCW tracks any state-attributed operations. The combination of operational AI deepfake deployment + monitoring vacuum + DNI omitting foreign interference from Worldwide Threats creates a compounding structural risk for November 2026.

flags.headline: US tariff-SCOTUS confrontation as rule of law and institutional resilience signal
flags.linkage: SCOTUS tariff ruling (Feb 20) and Trump's immediate Section 122 response are simultaneously a GMM event (macro-financial) and a WDM event (rule of law, institutional resilience). GMM tracks the economic coercion signal; WDM tracks the executive-judicial constraint dynamic. The pattern of executive defiance: IEEPA struck down → Section 122 deployed same day → 97% of SCOTUS emergency requests frame judicial review as illegitimate → Trump attended SCOTUS oral arguments (Apr 1, first sitting president ever). This is not episodic; it is a sustained campaign.

flags.headline: Georgia EU candidate state institutional collapse — ESA/WDM convergence
flags.linkage: Georgia's March 2026 three-law package (foreign grants criminalised, extremism law, 8-yr civil society ban) represents full institutional capture at the EU candidate state level. ESA tracks EU enlargement coherence and candidate state alignment; WDM tracks the democratic erosion. The Kobakhidze government's CPAC Hungary attendance (Mar 21) and alignment with Orbán's network confirms the cross-border authoritarian coordination signal tracked in WDM cms-002.

Moldova slipped into hybrid regime category in 2025, continuing Eastern European backsliding pattern with institutional constraints weakening.

signal_panel.headline: Hungary April 12 — the institutional democracy test of the decade
signal_panel.body: Six days from Hungary's parliamentary election, the most significant test of whether democratic transitions can occur within a captured electoral system. Independent polls give Tisza a 19–23 point lead among decided voters; Polymarket prices a Tisza win at 69%. But Fidesz has won every by-election since 2024 (8 consecutive), and the redistricted system requires Tisza to outperform by 3–6 points nationally for a majority. Three simultaneous confirmed Russian FIMI operations (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) are targeting the election — the highest documented pre-election FIMI density for any EU election. CSIS identifies four scenarios, including disputed results and delegitimisation campaigns. On the same day, Benin's presidential election is expected to confirm its crossing of the Rapid Decay threshold — with only ruling-bloc candidates approved. In the United States, the pattern of executive defiance of SCOTUS constraints continues: tariffs struck down on Feb 20, Section 122 deployed same day, and Trump attended SCOTUS oral arguments April 1 (a presidential first) as the court considered birthright citizenship reinterpretation by executive order.

weekly_brief:
Global democratic health enters its most consequential week of the 2026 cycle. The World Democracy Monitor carries 15 countries in Rapid Decay, 5 in Recovery, and 13 on the Watch List — the highest combined monitoring load since the monitor's launch. Two threshold events converge on April 12.

**Hungary: the decade's defining electoral test.** Six days from the parliamentary election, independent polling gives Péter Magyar's Tisza party a 19–23 point lead among decided voters (Medián: 58/35%; 21 Kutatóközpont: 56/37%; Polymarket: 69% Tisza). Leaked internal Fidesz data also projects a Tisza victory. The critical structural constraint remains: Hungary's redistricted mixed electoral system (106 single-member districts, 93 proportional seats) requires Tisza to outperform Fidesz by at least 3–6 percentage points nationally to achieve a parliamentary majority. Fidesz has won all 8 by-elections since the Tisza party emerged in 2024 — zero Tisza entries — demonstrating rural constituency resilience that national polling does not fully capture. Three simultaneous confirmed Russian information operations (SDA, Storm-1516, SVR Gamechanger) are targeting the election — the highest documented FIMI density for any single EU election (FCW intelligence-digest). CSIS (April 4) identifies four post-election scenarios, including disputed results with FIMI-driven delegitimisation. The WDM assesses: a Tisza majority would represent the most significant institutional democratic reversal within the EU since Hungary's illiberal turn began in 2010; a Fidesz retention despite record polling deficits would confirm that captured media environments and redistricted electoral architectures can permanently insulate incumbents from popular will within EU institutions. Hungary remains Rapid Decay (5.0↗) — score holds pending April 12 outcome.

**Benin: Watch List threshold crossing expected April 12.** On the same day as Hungary's election, Benin holds its presidential vote with only two candidates — both from the ruling bloc. Following January's parliamentary elections where all 109 National Assembly seats went to Talon-aligned parties (turnout 36.73%), CENA excluded all credible opposition candidates from the presidential race. CIVICUS March 2026 Watchlist rates Benin as 'Obstructed' — journalists arrested, media suspended, protests routinely banned. If Wadagni wins (expected), Benin crosses the WDM Rapid Decay threshold; any post-election crackdown on civil society would be the confirming structural signal. A Watch List → Rapid Decay upgrade will be processed in Issue 5.

**United States: judicial constraint under sustained pressure.** The executive-SCOTUS dynamic has entered a qualitatively new phase. After the Court's 6–3 ruling (Feb 20) struck down all IEEPA tariffs, the administration deployed Section 122 authority within hours (10–15% global surcharge), effectively replacing one emergency power with another. Trump became the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at SCOTUS (April 1) during the Trump v. Barbara case testing whether the 14th Amendment birthright citizenship clause can be reinterpreted by executive order — decision expected June/July. Reuters (March 7): 97% of Trump's 31 emergency SCOTUS requests frame judicial review as interference with presidential power — a systematic delegitimisation campaign. DNI Gabbard omitted foreign election interference from the Worldwide Threats hearing (March 19) — the first such omission in nearly a decade, structurally significant for the November 2026 midterms. US remains Rapid Decay (5.5↑).

**Georgia: legislative capture complete.** The March 2026 three-law package — criminalising foreign grants (6-yr sentences), an 'extremism against constitutional order' law, and an 8-year civil society ban for political participation — is fully in force. This is the most concentrated single-session legislative capture in any EU candidate country. FIDH has called for repeal; nine opposition parties have formed an alliance but are operationally constrained by the new extremism law itself. FCW confirmed that Georgia's Kobakhidze government is operationally coordinated with Hungary's Orbán government through the CPAC Hungary network (March 21). Georgia remains Rapid Decay (8.5↑).

**Global context.** Freedom House's Freedom in the World 2026 documents the 20th consecutive year of global democratic decline: 54 countries deteriorated vs 35 improved in 2025. V-Dem confirms autocracies now outnumber liberal democracies for the first time since the Cold War (92 vs 31), with 74% of the world's population living under autocratic systems. This is the structural environment in which the WDM's 15-country Rapid Decay roster operates.

Georgia judicial independence under pressure as part of broader democratic backsliding pattern.
Electoral system demonstrated resilience by rejecting Modi majority despite hate speech campaign and systemic repression, but integrity concerns persist.
